14	// LockRetryPolicy describes the retry/backoff curve used when attempting to
15	// acquire a process-shared lock that guards git index operations on the
16	// primary .git (the tracker-commit lock). The policy is owned by this
17	// package per ddx-da11a34a AC#4: callers in cli/internal/triage and
18	// elsewhere consult ActionRetryWithBackoff but do not duplicate the curve
19	// implementation here.
20	//
21	// The curve is exponential with a cap and a bounded max-retries budget so
22	// that lock contention is absorbed silently up to a point, after which it
23	// surfaces as an ordinary error to the caller. Per TD-031 §8.5
24	// (LockContentionContract) lock contention does NOT change persisted bead
25	// status; the bead remains claimable and the worker remains `draining`.
26	type LockRetryPolicy struct {
27		// InitialBackoff is the wait before the second attempt.
28		InitialBackoff time.Duration
29		// MaxBackoff caps the per-step backoff after exponential growth.
30		MaxBackoff time.Duration
31		// Multiplier is the per-step growth factor (>= 1.0).
32		Multiplier float64
33		// MaxRetries bounds the number of acquisition attempts after the
34		// initial try. Total attempts = MaxRetries + 1.
35		MaxRetries int
36		// MaxElapsed bounds the total wall-clock time spent retrying. If both
37		// MaxRetries and MaxElapsed are set, the first to trip wins.
38		MaxElapsed time.Duration
39	}
40	
41	// DefaultLockRetryPolicy returns the policy used by withTrackerLock. The
42	// curve is intentionally tight (sub-second) for in-process tracker-commit
43	// contention; the larger 30s/90s/300s curve referenced by the auto-triage
44	// child of ddx-3c154349 lives one layer up, in cli/internal/triage —
45	// it consults ActionRetryWithBackoff and re-queues across drain passes,
46	// while this policy resolves contention within a single attempt.
47	func DefaultLockRetryPolicy() LockRetryPolicy {
48		return LockRetryPolicy{
49			InitialBackoff: 50 * time.Millisecond,
50			MaxBackoff:     1 * time.Second,
51			Multiplier:     2.0,
52			MaxRetries:     200,
53			MaxElapsed:     30 * time.Second,
54		}
55	}
56	
57	// step returns the backoff duration for retry index n (0-based: n=0 is the
58	// wait BEFORE the second acquisition attempt).
59	func (p LockRetryPolicy) step(n int) time.Duration {
60		if p.InitialBackoff <= 0 {
61			return 0
62		}
63		d := float64(p.InitialBackoff)
64		m := p.Multiplier
65		if m < 1 {
66			m = 1
67		}
68		for i := 0; i < n; i++ {
69			d *= m
70			if p.MaxBackoff > 0 && d >= float64(p.MaxBackoff) {
71				d = float64(p.MaxBackoff)
72				break
73			}
74		}
75		out := time.Duration(d)
76		if p.MaxBackoff > 0 && out > p.MaxBackoff {
77			out = p.MaxBackoff
78		}
79		return out
80	}
81	
82	// trackerLockStaleAge is the age after which a held tracker lock is
83	// considered stale (owner crashed mid-commit) and may be forcibly broken.
84	var trackerLockStaleAge = 10 * time.Minute
85	
86	// trackerLockPolicy is the active retry policy for withTrackerLock.
87	// Tests may swap this out via the exported helpers below.
88	var trackerLockPolicy = DefaultLockRetryPolicy()
89	
90	// trackerLockPath returns the main-git lock directory for the given project
91	// root. The historical name remains because tests and older comments refer to
92	// the tracker lock, but the lock now protects all primary-checkout git writes:
93	// tracker commits, pre-dispatch checkpoint/ref updates, and landing.
94	func trackerLockPath(projectRoot string) string {
95		return ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, ".git-tracker.lock")
96	}
97	
98	// withMainGitLock acquires the process-shared main-git lock for the
99	// given project root, runs fn, and releases the lock. The lock is a
100	// directory created via os.Mkdir (atomic across processes on POSIX and
101	// Windows) following the same pattern as cli/internal/bead/lock.go.
102	//
103	// Contention is handled per LockRetryPolicy: exponential backoff up to a
104	// bounded budget. Per TD-031 §8.5 the contention itself does not change
105	// bead status; on budget exhaustion the caller surfaces an ordinary
106	// execution_failed and triage decides next steps.
107	func withMainGitLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
108		if projectRoot == "" {
109			return fn()
110		}
111		return withTrackerLockPolicy(projectRoot, trackerLockPolicy, fn)
112	}
113	
114	// WithMainGitLock exposes the process-shared main-git lock to sibling
115	// packages that need the same serialized add/commit boundary as execute-bead
116	// landing and pre-dispatch checkpointing.
117	func WithMainGitLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
118		return withMainGitLock(projectRoot, fn)
119	}
120	
121	// withTrackerLock is retained for existing tracker/pre-dispatch call sites. It
122	// uses the same process-shared lock as landing so separate ddx work processes
123	// cannot interleave commits, ref updates, and checkout sync in the main tree.
124	func withTrackerLock(projectRoot string, fn func() error) error {
125		return withMainGitLock(projectRoot, fn)
126	}

128	// TrackerLockSample carries timing and contention metrics for one
129	// withTrackerLock / withMainGitLock acquire+release cycle.
130	type TrackerLockSample struct {
131		LockDir string
132		Wait    time.Duration // time from entry to lock acquisition
133		Hold    time.Duration // time the callback held the lock
134		Retries int           // number of sleep-and-retry iterations (0 = acquired on first try)
135	}
136	
137	// TrackerLockMetricsSink is called after each successful tracker-lock
138	// acquire+release cycle. The zero value (nil) is a no-op. Tests may swap
139	// this to capture metrics.
140	var TrackerLockMetricsSink func(TrackerLockSample)
141	
142	// withTrackerLockPolicy is the policy-parameterised form of
143	// withTrackerLock; exposed at package scope so tests can pin a specific
144	// curve.
145	func withTrackerLockPolicy(projectRoot string, policy LockRetryPolicy, fn func() error) error {
146		lockDir := trackerLockPath(projectRoot)
147		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(lockDir), 0o755); err != nil {
148			return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock dir: %w", err)
149		}
150	
151		start := time.Now()
152		retries := 0
153		for attempt := 0; ; attempt++ {
154			err := os.Mkdir(lockDir, 0o755)
155			if err == nil {
156				_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid"),
157					[]byte(fmt.Sprintf("%d", os.Getpid())), 0o644)
158				_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "acquired_at"),
159					[]byte(time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)), 0o644)
160				break
161			}
162	
163			// Classify what exists at lockDir before deciding to sleep or fail.
164			// Per TD-031 §8.5: only a confirmed lock directory enters retry/backoff;
165			// malformed paths are operator diagnostics, not lock contention.
166			info, statErr := os.Lstat(lockDir)
167			if statErr != nil {
168				if os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
169					// Path disappeared between Mkdir and Lstat (race). Retry immediately.
170					continue
171				}
172				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock stat: %w", statErr)
173			}
174	
175			switch {
176			case info.Mode().IsDir():
177				// Real lock directory. Apply existing stale-owner policy.
178				if breakStaleTrackerLock(lockDir) {
179					continue
180				}
181				if attempt >= policy.MaxRetries {
182					return lockTimeoutError(lockDir, "max retries")
183				}
184				if policy.MaxElapsed > 0 && time.Since(start) >= policy.MaxElapsed {
185					return lockTimeoutError(lockDir, "max elapsed")
186				}
187				retries++
188				time.Sleep(policy.step(attempt))
189	
190			case info.Mode().IsRegular():
191				// Malformed: lock path must be a directory, not a regular file.
192				if time.Since(info.ModTime()) > trackerLockStaleAge {
193					// Stale regular file: remove with single-path removal and retry immediately.
194					if rmErr := os.Remove(lockDir); rmErr != nil && !os.IsNotExist(rmErr) {
195						return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: remove malformed stale file %s: %w", lockDir, rmErr)
196					}
197					continue
198				}
199				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: malformed lock path %s is a regular file; a lock directory was expected — remove it manually to recover", lockDir)
200	
201			default:
202				// Symlink, socket, device, or other special filesystem object. Do not remove.
203				return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock: malformed lock path %s has type %v; a lock directory was expected — inspect and remove it manually to recover", lockDir, info.Mode().Type())
204			}
205		}
206	
207		waitDur := time.Since(start)
208		holdStart := time.Now()
209		defer os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
210		fnErr := fn()
211		if sink := TrackerLockMetricsSink; sink != nil {
212			sink(TrackerLockSample{LockDir: lockDir, Wait: waitDur, Hold: time.Since(holdStart), Retries: retries})
213		}
214		return fnErr
215	}

217	func lockTimeoutError(lockDir, why string) error {
218		owner := "missing"
219		if pidData, perr := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid")); perr == nil && len(pidData) > 0 {
220			owner = strings.TrimSpace(string(pidData))
221		}
222		return fmt.Errorf("tracker lock timeout (%s, lock: %s, owner pid: %s)", why, lockDir, owner)
223	}
224	
225	// breakStaleTrackerLock removes lockDir if its owner process is dead or the
226	// lock is older than trackerLockStaleAge. Returns true if the lock was broken.
227	func breakStaleTrackerLock(lockDir string) bool {
228		pidData, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "pid"))
229		if err == nil {
230			pid, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(string(pidData)))
231			if err == nil && pid > 0 && pid != os.Getpid() {
232				if !trackerProcessAlive(pid) {
233					os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
234					return true
235				}
236			}
237		}
238	
239		acquiredData, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(lockDir, "acquired_at"))
240		if err == nil {
241			acquired, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, strings.TrimSpace(string(acquiredData)))
242			if err == nil && time.Since(acquired) > trackerLockStaleAge {
243				os.RemoveAll(lockDir)
244				return true
245			}
246		}
247	
248		return false
249	}

3	// execute_bead_land.go — the land coordinator pattern.
4	//
5	// This file implements the human-PR landing model for execute-bead results.
6	// The old Merge() path in execute_bead_orchestrator.go has been deleted; all
7	// target-ref writes now flow through Land(), which is called exactly once per
8	// submission by a per-project coordinator goroutine (see
9	// internal/server/workers.go:LandCoordinator).
10	//
11	// The flow mirrors how a human merges PRs:
12	//
13	//   1. Read the current target tip from the local branch.
14	//   2. If the current tip equals the worker's BaseRev — fast-forward the
15	//      target branch directly to the worker's ResultRev via update-ref. The
16	//      worker's commit keeps its original parent; no new commit is created.
17	//   3. Otherwise — the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
18	//      a temporary detached worktree at the current target tip, run
19	//      `git merge --no-ff` to introduce the worker's ResultRev, and
20	//      fast-forward the target branch to the resulting merge commit. The
21	//      worker's commit is NEVER rewritten: its parent is still BaseRev, so
22	//      a later replay observes the same inputs the worker saw.
23	//   4. If the merge conflicts — abort cleanly, preserve the original
24	//      ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>,
25	//      and return preserved status. Target branch is never modified.
26	//   5. If evidence needs to be folded in, stage and commit it locally, then
27	//      sync the operator checkout to the new head when safe to do so.
28	//
29	// Network sync is intentionally out of scope for Land(): no fetch and no
30	// push happen here. Operators sync origin with raw git commands after the
31	// drain session completes.
32	//
33	// Replay fidelity is the reason for merge-over-rebase: a rebased commit has
34	// a rewritten parent that lies about what the worker saw at execution time.
35	// A merge commit preserves both histories — the worker's original commit
36	// (parent = BaseRev) and the target's prior tip — losslessly.
37	//
38	// The in-process coordinator serializes submissions inside one process. Land()
39	// also takes the process-shared main-git lock so separate `ddx work` processes
40	// cannot interleave target-ref updates, evidence commits, or checkout sync.
41	
42	import (
43		"bytes"
44		"context"
45		"encoding/json"
46		"fmt"
47		"go/parser"
48		"go/token"
49		"os"
50		"os/exec"
51		"path/filepath"
52		"strconv"
53		"strings"
54		"time"
55	
56		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
57		internalgit "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
58	)
59	
60	const defaultLargeDeletionLineThreshold = 200
61	
62	// LandRequest is one submission to the land coordinator: "here is the worker's
63	// result from base B to result R for bead X; land it on the project's target
64	// branch."
65	type LandRequest struct {
66		// WorktreeDir is the path to the project's repository directory (the
67		// directory the coordinator operates on). The original worker worktree
68		// has typically already been removed by the time Land() runs — Land()
69		// creates its own temporary worktrees when a merge is needed.
70		WorktreeDir string
71	
72		// BaseRev is the revision the worker branched off when it started.
73		// When the current target tip equals BaseRev, Land() takes the fast path.
74		BaseRev string
75	
76		// ResultRev is the worker's final commit SHA. Must be reachable in the
77		// project's git object store at the time Land() is called.
78		ResultRev string
79	
80		// BeadID identifies the bead this submission is for. Used to build the
81		// preserve-ref path on conflict.
82		BeadID string
83	
84		// AttemptID uniquely identifies this land attempt. Used to build the
85		// preserve-ref path on conflict so concurrent attempts for the same
86		// bead do not collide.
87		AttemptID string
88	
89		// TargetBranch is the branch to advance. When empty, Land() resolves
90		// the project's current HEAD branch and uses that.
91		TargetBranch string
92	
93		// EvidenceDir is the relative path to the per-attempt execution evidence
94		// directory (e.g. ".ddx/executions/20260416T181205-b5419982"). When
95		// non-empty and the main land succeeds, Land() creates a trailing
96		// evidence commit that folds these files into the target branch. The
97		// agent's commit SHA is NOT amended — the evidence commit is a separate
98		// child commit, preserving closing_commit_sha references.
99		EvidenceDir string
100	
101		// PostLandCommand is an optional project verification command run after
102		// Land() advances the local target ref and syncs the worktree, but before
103		// evidence commit creation or checkout sync. A failure restores the target
104		// ref to its pre-land SHA and preserves ResultRev under refs/ddx/iterations.
105		PostLandCommand []string
106	
107		// LargeDeletionLineThreshold overrides the default per-file deletion gate
108		// threshold. Zero or negative uses defaultLargeDeletionLineThreshold.
109		LargeDeletionLineThreshold int
110	}
111	
112	// LandResult describes the outcome of a single Land() call.
113	type LandResult struct {
114		// Status is one of:
115		//   - "landed":    the target branch now points at a new commit
116		//                  (either ResultRev itself on the fast-forward path,
117		//                  or a merge commit on the merge path).
118		//   - "preserved": the merge conflicted; ResultRev is saved under
119		//                  PreserveRef and the target branch is unchanged.
120		//   - "no-changes": ResultRev == BaseRev; nothing to land.
121		Status string
122	
123		// NewTip is the new value of the target branch when Status == "landed".
124		// On the fast-forward path NewTip == ResultRev; on the merge path NewTip
125		// is the SHA of the merge commit (whose parents are the prior target
126		// tip and ResultRev). Empty when preserved or no-changes.
127		NewTip string
128	
129		// LandedTip is the target branch tip immediately after the implementation
130		// (or already-committed evidence bundle) lands, but before any trailing
131		// audit-only evidence/final-result commit. When no trailing commit is
132		// created it matches NewTip.
133		LandedTip string
134	
135		// TargetBranch is the resolved branch name that Land() advanced or attempted
136		// to advance. It is set on landed and preserved results so callers can make
137		// branch-local recovery explicit in terminal output and evidence.
138		TargetBranch string
139	
140		// Merged is true when the land took the merge-commit path (current tip
141		// had advanced beyond BaseRev, so Land() created a merge commit to
142		// combine the worker's result with the new target tip). False on the
143		// fast-forward path where the worker's commit became the new tip
144		// unchanged.
145		Merged bool
146	
147		// PreserveRef is set when Status == "preserved". It names the ref under
148		// refs/ddx/iterations/ where ResultRev was saved for later recovery.
149		PreserveRef string
150	
151		// Reason is a human-readable explanation, especially useful when
152		// Status == "preserved" (e.g. "merge conflict").
153		Reason string
154	
155		// MergedCommitCount is the number of commits reachable from ResultRev but
156		// not from BaseRev — i.e. the "size" of the worker's contribution being
157		// merged in. Zero on the no-changes path. Set on both the fast-forward
158		// and merge-commit paths so metrics can compare contribution sizes.
159		MergedCommitCount int
160	
161		// EvidenceCommitSHA is set when a trailing execution-evidence commit was
162		// created after the main land. When set, NewTip points at this commit
163		// (not at the original agent commit or merge commit).
164		EvidenceCommitSHA string
165	
166		// CheckoutSyncDeferred is true when the target ref advanced but DDx left
167		// the operator checkout files untouched because dirty local paths overlap
168		// files changed by the new target tip.
169		CheckoutSyncDeferred bool
170	
171		// CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths lists the dirty paths that caused checkout sync
172		// to be skipped.
173		CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths []string
174	}

176	// PreClaimResult is the outcome of a FetchOriginAncestryCheck call.
177	type PreClaimResult struct {
178		// Action is one of:
179		//   "unchanged"     — local tip == origin tip, no action taken
180		//   "fast-forwarded"— local was behind origin; local branch was advanced
181		//   "no-origin"     — no origin remote; check skipped
182		//   "local-ahead"   — local is ahead of origin; no action needed
183		//   "diverged"      — neither is ancestor of the other; claim should be aborted
184		Action    string
185		LocalSHA  string
186		OriginSHA string
187	}

189	// IsIgnorableFetchOriginError reports whether a pre-claim ancestry-check
190	// failure came from the best-effort network fetch. Local worktree safety
191	// failures must propagate so workers do not claim work from an unsafe trunk.
192	func IsIgnorableFetchOriginError(err error) bool {
193		if err == nil {
194			return false
195		}
196		msg := err.Error()
197		return strings.HasPrefix(msg, "git fetch origin ") || strings.HasPrefix(msg, "git fetch origin:")
198	}

200	// LandingGitOps abstracts the git operations Land() needs. RealLandingGitOps
201	// shells out to git; tests inject fakes or run against real temp repos.
202	type LandingGitOps interface {
203		// CurrentBranch returns the branch HEAD currently points at in dir, or
204		// an error if HEAD is detached or unresolvable.
205		CurrentBranch(dir string) (string, error)
206	
207		// ResolveRef resolves ref (e.g. "refs/heads/main" or "origin/main") to a
208		// commit SHA. When ref is unresolvable returns ("", error).
209		ResolveRef(dir, ref string) (string, error)
210	
211		// UpdateRefTo updates ref in dir to sha. When oldSHA is non-empty, the
212		// update is conditional on the current ref value equalling oldSHA.
213		UpdateRefTo(dir, ref, sha, oldSHA string) error
214	
215		// SyncWorkTreeToHead syncs the index AND the working-tree files in dir
216		// to HEAD after a non-checkout ref update (e.g. update-ref). fromRev is
217		// the commit HEAD pointed at BEFORE the ref update; it is used to
218		// compute the minimal set of tracked files changed by the update so
219		// that unrelated local modifications (agent-logs, beads.jsonl heartbeat
220		// writes, operator scratch) are NOT clobbered.
221		//
222		// Needed because Land() advances the target ref via update-ref, which
223		// touches neither the index nor the worktree. Before this fix, Land()
224		// only ran `git read-tree HEAD` to sync the index — leaving the main
225		// worktree showing phantom deleted/modified entries for every file the
226		// landed commit touched, and subsequent agents reading files from disk
227		// would see stale content.
228		//
229		// Implementation: `git read-tree HEAD` + `git diff --name-only fromRev
230		// HEAD` to enumerate changed files + `git checkout-index -f --` to
231		// materialize them from the freshly-synced index.
232		SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev string) error
233	
234		// AddWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out at rev in dir.
235		AddWorktree(dir, path, rev string) error
236	
237		// AddBranchWorktree creates a new worktree at path checked out on branch
238		// in dir. It is used for clean landing finalization when the operator
239		// checkout already has the target branch checked out.
240		AddBranchWorktree(dir, path, branch string) error
241	
242		// RemoveWorktree removes the worktree at path in dir (force).
243		RemoveWorktree(dir, path string) error
244	
245		// MergeInto runs `git merge --no-ff -m msg srcRev` inside wtDir, which
246		// must already be checked out at the current target tip. A clean merge
247		// produces a merge commit whose parents are [currentTip, srcRev]; the
248		// commit SHA can be read with HeadRevAt. Returns nil on clean merge,
249		// or an error on conflict. On error, the implementation is responsible
250		// for running `git merge --abort` so the worktree is left clean.
251		MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error
252	
253		// HeadRevAt returns HEAD of the git workdir at dir.
254		HeadRevAt(dir string) (string, error)
255	
256		// CountCommits returns the number of commits reachable from tip but not
257		// from base (i.e. git rev-list --count base..tip). Used to record the
258		// contribution size in land metrics. Returns 0 on error.
259		CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int
260	
261		// StageDir stages all files under relPath in dir for the next commit.
262		StageDir(dir, relPath string) error
263	
264		// CommitStaged creates a commit from currently staged changes using msg
265		// as the commit message. Returns (sha, nil) when a commit was made,
266		// ("", nil) when nothing was staged, and ("", err) on failure.
267		CommitStaged(dir, msg string) (string, error)
268	
269		// DiffNumstat returns the output of `git diff --numstat base tip --` in
270		// dir. Used by the large-deletion gate. Returning ("", nil) indicates no
271		// diffable output (e.g. in a test stub with no real repo).
272		DiffNumstat(dir, base, tip string) (string, error)
273	
274		// DiffNameOnly returns the list of changed file paths between base and tip
275		// in dir (`git diff --name-only base tip --`). Used by the syntax-sanity
276		// gate. Returning (nil, nil) indicates no changes.
277		DiffNameOnly(dir, base, tip string) ([]string, error)
278	}

314	// alwaysSkipSyncPaths lists live-state files governed by bead.Store WithLock.
315	// The committed worktree version is a stale snapshot of tracker state at claim
316	// time; the main-worktree copy is always authoritative and must never be
317	// overwritten by SyncWorkTreeToHead regardless of dirty-before-land state.
318	var alwaysSkipSyncPaths = []string{
319		".ddx/beads.jsonl",
320		".ddx/beads-archive.jsonl",
321	}

324		return syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev, alwaysSkipSyncPaths)

327	// readTreeHeadWithRetry runs `git read-tree HEAD` in dir, retrying with
328	// exponential backoff when the git index lock is held by a concurrent process.
329	//
330	// Background (ddx-7e659c95): git read-tree needs to acquire .git/index.lock.
331	// When an operator git command (git status, git commit, etc.) holds the lock
332	// concurrently, git exits 128 with "Unable to create index.lock: File exists".
333	// Without retry the main worktree index stays dirty after a merge landing,
334	// causing a subsequent `git commit beads.jsonl` to sweep in phantom reverts of
335	// the bead's changes.
336	//
337	// The retry budget (30 s, 100 ms → 1 s backoff) matches the
338	// DefaultLockRetryPolicy used by withTrackerLock so contention handling is
339	// consistent across all ddx git-index operations.
340	func readTreeHeadWithRetry(dir string) ([]byte, error) {
341		const (
342			initialBackoff = 100 * time.Millisecond
343			maxBackoff     = 1 * time.Second
344			maxElapsed     = 30 * time.Second
345		)
346		start := time.Now()
347		backoff := initialBackoff
348		var lastDiag string
349		for {
350			out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "read-tree", "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
351			if err == nil {
352				return out, nil
353			}
354			// Only retry on index lock contention; all other errors are fatal.
355			if !strings.Contains(string(out), "index.lock") {
356				return out, err
357			}
358			// Identify the lock owner and break the lock if it is stale or
359			// owned by a dead process. This converts a hard 30s wait into an
360			// instant recovery for the common crashed-git case.
361			if result, recErr := recoverGitIndexLock(dir); recErr == nil {
362				lastDiag = result.Reason
363				if result.Removed {
364					// Lock cleared — retry immediately.
365					continue
366				}
367			}
368			if time.Since(start) >= maxElapsed {
369				if lastDiag != "" {
370					return out, fmt.Errorf("%s; index-lock owner: %s: %w",
371						strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), lastDiag, err)
372				}
373				return out, err
374			}
375			time.Sleep(backoff)
376			backoff *= 2
377			if backoff > maxBackoff {
378				backoff = maxBackoff
379			}
380		}
381	}
382	
383	func syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev string, skipPaths []string) error {
384		skipWorktreePaths, err := checkpointSkipWorktreePaths(dir)
385		if err != nil {
386			return err
387		}
388	
389		// Step 1: sync the index to HEAD. This is required before checkout-index
390		// below will do anything useful, and also keeps subsequent CommitTracker
391		// calls from building stale trees.
392		//
393		// ddx-7e659c95: git read-tree requires .git/index.lock. If an operator
394		// git command (git status, git commit, etc.) holds the lock concurrently,
395		// git read-tree exits 128 with "Unable to create index.lock: File exists".
396		// Retry with exponential backoff for up to 30 s so transient operator
397		// contention does not leave the main worktree index dirty.
398		out, err := readTreeHeadWithRetry(dir)
399		if err != nil {
400			return fmt.Errorf("git read-tree HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
401		}
402		if err := restoreCheckpointSkipWorktreePaths(dir, skipWorktreePaths); err != nil {
403			return err
404		}
405	
406		// Step 2: compute the list of tracked files changed between the previous
407		// HEAD and the current HEAD. These are the files that the landing commit
408		// added, modified, or deleted. We only restore THESE files to avoid
409		// clobbering unrelated local modifications (agent-logs being written by
410		// the running server, beads.jsonl heartbeat updates, operator scratch).
411		if fromRev == "" {
412			// No previous HEAD known — fall back to the unsafe behaviour of
413			// read-tree only. Acceptable when the caller is a best-effort path
414			// that cannot reconstruct fromRev.
415			return nil
416		}
417		diffOut, diffErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
418		if diffErr != nil {
419			// Diff failed (bad fromRev, shallow history, etc.) — leave the
420			// worktree stale rather than risk a broken checkout. The CommitTracker
421			// stale-tree bug is the prior status quo and no worse than before.
422			return nil
423		}
424		changed := strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut)))
425		if len(changed) == 0 {
426			return nil
427		}
428	
429		// Step 3: split into existing-in-HEAD (checkout-index) and
430		// deleted-in-HEAD (os.Remove) buckets. checkout-index only writes files
431		// that are in the index; it cannot represent a deletion, so we handle
432		// those ourselves.
433		skip := map[string]bool{}
434		for _, path := range skipPaths {
435			skip[filepath.ToSlash(path)] = true
436		}
437		for _, path := range skipWorktreePaths {
438			skip[filepath.ToSlash(path)] = true
439		}
440		var indexFiles []string
441		var removedFiles []string
442		for _, f := range changed {
443			if skip[filepath.ToSlash(f)] {
444				continue
445			}
446			probe := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "ls-files", "--error-unmatch", "--", f)
447			if probe.Run() == nil {
448				indexFiles = append(indexFiles, f)
449			} else {
450				removedFiles = append(removedFiles, f)
451			}
452		}
453	
454		// Step 4: materialize the index-present files into the working tree.
455		// -f overwrites any stale content at these exact paths. Unrelated files
456		// are untouched because we pass the specific path list.
457		if len(indexFiles) > 0 {
458			for _, f := range indexFiles {
459				if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(filepath.Join(dir, f)), 0o755); err != nil {
460					return fmt.Errorf("creating checkout parent for %s: %w", f, err)
461				}
462			}
463			args := []string{"checkout-index", "-f", "--"}
464			args = append(args, indexFiles...)
465			out2, err2 := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
466			if err2 != nil {
467				return fmt.Errorf("git checkout-index -f: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out2)), err2)
468			}
469		}
470	
471		// Step 5: remove files that the landing commit deleted and whose removal
472		// did not propagate to the worktree because update-ref bypassed the
473		// working-tree update.
474		for _, f := range removedFiles {
475			full := filepath.Join(dir, f)
476			_ = os.Remove(full) // best-effort; leave the file if removal fails
477		}
478	
479		return nil
480	}

482	func syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps LandingGitOps, dir, fromRev string, dirtyBefore []string, result *LandResult) {
483		overlap, err := checkoutSyncDirtyOverlapPaths(dir, fromRev, dirtyBefore)
484		if err == nil && len(overlap) > 0 {
485			if result != nil {
486				result.CheckoutSyncDeferred = true
487				result.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths = appendUniqueStrings(result.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths, overlap...)
488			}
489			_ = syncWorkTreeToHeadExcludingPaths(dir, fromRev, overlap)
490			return
491		}
492		_ = gitOps.SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, fromRev)
493	}

495	func ensureLandingWorktreeReady(dir, targetBranch string) error {
496		if !isInsideGitWorktree(dir) {
497			return nil
498		}
499		branchOut, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "branch", "--show-current").CombinedOutput()
500		if err != nil {
501			return fmt.Errorf("checking landing branch: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(branchOut)), err)
502		}
503		branch := strings.TrimSpace(string(branchOut))
504		if branch == "" {
505			return fmt.Errorf("landing worktree is detached; expected branch %q", targetBranch)
506		}
507		if targetBranch != "" && branch != targetBranch {
508			return fmt.Errorf("landing branch mismatch: on %q, want %q", branch, targetBranch)
509		}
510		return waitForEmptyGitIndex(dir, 2*time.Second)
511	}

513	func isInsideGitWorktree(dir string) bool {
514		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--is-inside-work-tree").CombinedOutput()
515		return err == nil && strings.TrimSpace(string(out)) == "true"
516	}
517	
518	func waitForEmptyGitIndex(dir string, timeout time.Duration) error {
519		deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
520		repairedCorruptIndex := false
521		for {
522			out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--quiet").CombinedOutput()
523			if err == nil {
524				return nil
525			}
526			if !repairedCorruptIndex && isRecoverableLandingIndexCorruption(string(out)) {
527				repairedCorruptIndex = true
528				if _, recErr := readTreeHeadWithRetry(dir); recErr != nil {
529					return fmt.Errorf("repairing landing worktree index: %w", recErr)
530				}
531				if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--quiet").Run(); err == nil {
532					return nil
533				}
534			}
535			if time.Now().After(deadline) {
536				// The index drifted from HEAD. Two causes are possible:
537				//   1. A prior land's post-merge SyncWorkTreeToHead failed
538				//      (e.g. transient .git/index.lock contention) and its
539				//      error was swallowed by the best-effort caller. The
540				//      index now matches a recent ancestor of HEAD —
541				//      reverting the most recent merges. Recovering by
542				//      running read-tree HEAD is safe and unblocks the queue.
543				//   2. The operator staged real work that does not match any
544				//      ancestor tree. Recovery here would silently destroy
545				//      their changes — refuse, surface the error, and let
546				//      the operator resolve it.
547				if matches, _ := indexMatchesRecentAncestorTree(dir, 20); matches {
548					if _, recErr := readTreeHeadWithRetry(dir); recErr == nil {
549						if err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--quiet").Run(); err == nil {
550							return nil
551						}
552					}
553				}
554				stagedOut, _ := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--cached", "--name-status").CombinedOutput()
555				staged := strings.TrimSpace(string(stagedOut))
556				if staged == "" {
557					staged = "unknown staged changes"
558				}
559				return fmt.Errorf("landing worktree has staged changes after waiting %s:\n%s", timeout, staged)
560			}
561			time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
562		}
563	}
564	
565	func isRecoverableLandingIndexCorruption(output string) bool {
566		lower := strings.ToLower(output)
567		return strings.Contains(lower, "index file smaller than expected") ||
568			strings.Contains(lower, "bad index file") ||
569			strings.Contains(lower, "unexpected end of file while reading index")
570	}
571	
572	// indexMatchesRecentAncestorTree reports whether the current index's tree
573	// matches the tree of any of the last `maxDepth` ancestors of HEAD. A
574	// match means the index reflects a state we have already advanced past —
575	// the signature of a swallowed SyncWorkTreeToHead failure — and is
576	// therefore safe to overwrite by re-reading HEAD into the index.
577	//
578	// Operator-staged work introduces novel tree contents that will not
579	// match any historical tree, so the heuristic preserves their work.
580	func indexMatchesRecentAncestorTree(dir string, maxDepth int) (bool, error) {
581		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "write-tree").Output()
582		if err != nil {
583			return false, err
584		}
585		indexTree := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
586		if indexTree == "" {
587			return false, nil
588		}
589		for depth := 1; depth <= maxDepth; depth++ {
590			ref := fmt.Sprintf("HEAD~%d^{tree}", depth)
591			treeOut, treeErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref).Output()
592			if treeErr != nil {
593				return false, nil
594			}
595			if strings.TrimSpace(string(treeOut)) == indexTree {
596				return true, nil
597			}
598		}
599		return false, nil
600	}
601	
602	func checkoutSyncDirtyOverlapPaths(dir, fromRev string, dirtyPaths []string) ([]string, error) {
603		if fromRev == "" || len(dirtyPaths) == 0 {
604			return nil, nil
605		}
606		diffOut, diffErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "diff", "--name-only", fromRev, "HEAD").CombinedOutput()
607		if diffErr != nil {
608			return nil, diffErr
609		}
610		changed := map[string]bool{}
611		for _, path := range strings.Fields(strings.TrimSpace(string(diffOut))) {
612			changed[filepath.ToSlash(path)] = true
613		}
614		if len(changed) == 0 {
615			return nil, nil
616		}
617		var overlap []string
618		for _, path := range dirtyPaths {
619			slashPath := filepath.ToSlash(path)
620			if checkoutSyncDeferralIgnoredPath(slashPath) {
621				continue
622			}
623			if changed[slashPath] {
624				overlap = append(overlap, slashPath)
625			}
626		}
627		return overlap, nil
628	}
629	
630	func checkoutSyncDeferralIgnoredPath(path string) bool {
631		if strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/executions/") ||
632			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/runs/") ||
633			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/backups/") ||
634			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/run-state/") ||
635			strings.HasPrefix(path, ".ddx/.git-tracker.lock/") {
636			return true
637		}
638		return path == ".ddx/run-state.json" || path == ExecutionCleanupMetadataFileName
639	}
640	
641	func appendUniqueStrings(existing []string, additions ...string) []string {
642		seen := make(map[string]bool, len(existing)+len(additions))
643		for _, value := range existing {
644			seen[value] = true
645		}
646		for _, value := range additions {
647			if seen[value] {
648				continue
649			}
650			seen[value] = true
651			existing = append(existing, value)
652		}
653		return existing
654	}

679	func (RealLandingGitOps) MergeInto(wtDir, srcRev, msg string) error {
680		// --no-ff forces a merge commit even when the merge could fast-forward
681		// (which shouldn't happen given our caller's ancestry check, but is a
682		// defensive guarantee that target history always gets a marker commit).
683		// We inject user.name/user.email via -c so the merge commit can be
684		// created even when the worktree inherited no git config; the
685		// coordinator is a machine actor and should not adopt a human's identity.
686		out, err := internalgit.Command(
687			context.Background(), wtDir,
688			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
689			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
690			"merge", "--no-ff", "-m", msg, srcRev,
691		).CombinedOutput()
692		if err != nil {
693			_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wtDir, "merge", "--abort").Run()
694			return fmt.Errorf("git merge --no-ff %s: %s: %w", srcRev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
695		}
696		return nil
697	}

701		if err != nil {
702			return "", fmt.Errorf("git rev-parse HEAD: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
703		}
704		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), nil
705	}

707	func (RealLandingGitOps) CountCommits(dir, base, tip string) int {
708		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-list", "--count", base+".."+tip).CombinedOutput()
709		if err != nil {
710			return 0
711		}
712		s := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
713		n := 0
714		for _, c := range s {
715			if c >= '0' && c <= '9' {
716				n = n*10 + int(c-'0')
717			}
718		}

782	// by the CLI and server preclaim hooks.
783	func (RealLandingGitOps) FetchOriginAncestryCheck(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error) {
784		var result PreClaimResult
785		err := withMainGitLock(dir, func() error {
786			var checkErr error
787			result, checkErr = fetchOriginAncestryCheckLocked(dir, targetBranch)
788			return checkErr

792	
793	func fetchOriginAncestryCheckLocked(dir, targetBranch string) (PreClaimResult, error) {
794		if err := ensureLandingWorktreeReady(dir, targetBranch); err != nil {
795			return PreClaimResult{}, err
796		}
797	
798		// Step 1: check for origin remote.
799		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "remote", "get-url", "origin").CombinedOutput()
800		if err != nil {
801			// No origin remote — single-machine case; skip check.
802			return PreClaimResult{Action: "no-origin"}, nil
803		}
804		_ = out // remote URL not needed
805	
806		// Step 2: fetch origin/targetBranch (best-effort; failure is non-fatal but surfaced).
807		if fetchOut, fetchErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "fetch", "origin", targetBranch).CombinedOutput(); fetchErr != nil {
808			if trimmed := strings.TrimSpace(string(fetchOut)); trimmed != "" {
809				return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("git fetch origin %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, trimmed, fetchErr)
810			}
811			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("git fetch origin %s: %w", targetBranch, fetchErr)
812		}
813	
814		// Step 3: resolve local tip.
815		localOut, localErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/heads/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
816		if localErr != nil {
817			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving local %s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut)), localErr)
818		}
819		localSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(localOut))
820	
821		// Step 4: resolve origin tip.
822		originOut, originErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "rev-parse", "--verify", "refs/remotes/origin/"+targetBranch).CombinedOutput()
823		if originErr != nil {
824			return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("resolving origin/%s: %s: %w", targetBranch, strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut)), originErr)
825		}
826		originSHA := strings.TrimSpace(string(originOut))
827	
828		// Step 5: compare.
829		if localSHA == originSHA {
830			return PreClaimResult{Action: "unchanged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
831		}
832	
833		// Is local an ancestor of origin? (origin is ahead)
834		localAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", localSHA, originSHA).Run()
835		if localAncestorErr == nil {
836			// Origin is ahead: fast-forward local branch via update-ref + sync worktree.
837			targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
838			if upErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "update-ref", targetRef, originSHA, localSHA).Run(); upErr != nil {
839				return PreClaimResult{}, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, originSHA, upErr)
840			}
841			// Sync index + working tree to new HEAD so the main worktree files
842			// reflect the pulled-down origin commits. Pass localSHA as fromRev
843			// to restrict the restore to files actually changed by origin's
844			// advance, preserving unrelated local modifications.
845			_ = (RealLandingGitOps{}).SyncWorkTreeToHead(dir, localSHA)
846			return PreClaimResult{Action: "fast-forwarded", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
847		}
848	
849		// Is origin an ancestor of local? (local is ahead)
850		originAncestorErr := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), dir, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", originSHA, localSHA).Run()
851		if originAncestorErr == nil {
852			return PreClaimResult{Action: "local-ahead", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
853		}
854	
855		// Neither is ancestor of the other: diverged.
856		return PreClaimResult{Action: "diverged", LocalSHA: localSHA, OriginSHA: originSHA}, nil
857	}

859	// landIterationRef returns the documented hidden ref for a land-time preserve.
860	// Format: refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<attempt-id>-<short-tip>. The short-tip
861	// captures the current target tip at the time of the conflict so subsequent
862	// recovery tools can reconstruct which merge target was in play.
863	func landIterationRef(beadID, attemptID, tip string) string {
864		short := tip
865		if len(short) > 12 {
866			short = short[:12]
867		}
868		attempt := attemptID
869		if attempt == "" {
870			attempt = NowFunc().UTC().Format("20060102T150405Z")
871		}
872		return fmt.Sprintf("refs/ddx/iterations/%s/%s-%s", beadID, attempt, short)
873	}

875	// landEvidence creates a trailing commit that folds the per-attempt execution
876	// evidence directory into the target branch. Called after the main land (ff or
877	// merge) succeeds and before checkout sync. The evidence commit is a normal
878	// child of result.NewTip — the agent's original commit SHA is not amended.
879	func landEvidence(wd, targetBranch string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps, result *LandResult) error {
880		branch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
881		if err != nil {
882			return fmt.Errorf("evidence commit branch check: %w", err)
883		}
884		if branch != targetBranch {
885			return fmt.Errorf("evidence commit branch mismatch: on %q, want %q", branch, targetBranch)
886		}
887		if err := gitOps.StageDir(wd, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
888			return fmt.Errorf("stage evidence: %w", err)
889		}
890		msg := fmt.Sprintf("chore: add execution evidence [%s]", shortAttempt(req.AttemptID))
891		sha, err := gitOps.CommitStaged(wd, msg)
892		if err != nil {
893			return fmt.Errorf("commit evidence: %w", err)
894		}
895		if sha == "" {
896			// Evidence already committed in the working tree (worktree-origin path):
897			// the bundle was committed inside the attempt worktree as part of ResultRev
898			// so it is present at HEAD when the landing finalization worktree is checked
899			// out. No trailing commit needed; verify tracked files exist then accept.
900			if !evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles(wd, req.EvidenceDir) {
901				return fmt.Errorf("commit evidence: no staged evidence files under %s", req.EvidenceDir)
902			}
903			headSHA, headErr := gitOps.HeadRevAt(wd)
904			if headErr != nil {
905				return fmt.Errorf("evidence already committed, reading HEAD: %w", headErr)
906			}
907			result.EvidenceCommitSHA = headSHA
908			return nil
909		}
910		result.EvidenceCommitSHA = sha
911		result.NewTip = sha
912		return nil
913	}

996	func copyEvidenceDirForLanding(projectRoot, landingWD, relPath string) error {
997		if relPath == "" || sameFilesystemPath(projectRoot, landingWD) {
998			return nil
999		}
1000		cleanRel, ok := cleanRelativePath(relPath)
1001		if !ok {
1002			return fmt.Errorf("invalid evidence path %q", relPath)
1003		}
1004		if evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles(landingWD, cleanRel) {
1005			return nil

1007		src := filepath.Join(projectRoot, cleanRel)
1008		dst := filepath.Join(landingWD, cleanRel)
1009		info, err := os.Stat(src)
1010		if err != nil {
1011			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
1012				return nil
1013			}
1014			return err
1015		}
1016		if !info.IsDir() {
1017			return nil
1018		}
1019		if err := os.RemoveAll(dst); err != nil {
1020			return err
1021		}
1022		return filepath.WalkDir(src, func(path string, d os.DirEntry, err error) error {
1023			if err != nil {
1024				return err
1025			}
1026			rel, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
1027			if err != nil {
1028				return err
1029			}
1030			target := filepath.Join(dst, rel)
1031			if d.IsDir() {
1032				return os.MkdirAll(target, 0o755)
1033			}
1034			info, err := d.Info()
1035			if err != nil {
1036				return err
1037			}
1038			data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
1039			if err != nil {
1040				return err
1041			}
1042			return os.WriteFile(target, data, info.Mode().Perm())
1043		})
1044	}

1069	func cleanRelativePath(path string) (string, bool) {
1070		if path == "" || filepath.IsAbs(path) {
1071			return "", false
1072		}
1073		clean := filepath.Clean(filepath.FromSlash(path))
1074		if clean == "." || strings.HasPrefix(clean, ".."+string(os.PathSeparator)) || clean == ".." {
1075			return "", false
1076		}
1077		return clean, true
1078	}

1080	func sameFilesystemPath(a, b string) bool {
1081		if a == "" || b == "" {
1082			return false
1083		}
1084		absA, err := filepath.Abs(a)
1085		if err != nil {
1086			absA = filepath.Clean(a)
1087		}
1088		absB, err := filepath.Abs(b)
1089		if err != nil {
1090			absB = filepath.Clean(b)
1091		}
1092		if resolvedA, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(absA); err == nil {
1093			absA = resolvedA
1094		}
1095		if resolvedB, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(absB); err == nil {
1096			absB = resolvedB
1097		}
1098		return absA == absB
1099	}

1101	// Land performs the local land flow for a single submission.
1102	// It serializes primary-checkout git operations with the process-shared
1103	// main-git lock so separate ddx work processes cannot interleave landing with
1104	// tracker commits or pre-dispatch checkpoint/ref updates.
1105	//
1106	// projectRoot is the directory containing the project's .git. req.WorktreeDir,
1107	// when non-empty, is used as the git working directory for all commands; when
1108	// empty, projectRoot is used. Both usually refer to the same dir — the field
1109	// is kept for forward compatibility with multi-clone topologies.
1110	func Land(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
1111		var result *LandResult
1112		err := withMainGitLock(projectRoot, func() error {
1113			var landErr error
1114			result, landErr = landLocked(projectRoot, req, gitOps)
1115			return landErr
1116		})
1117		return result, err
1118	}
1119	
1120	func landLocked(projectRoot string, req LandRequest, gitOps LandingGitOps) (*LandResult, error) {
1121		if gitOps == nil {
1122			gitOps = RealLandingGitOps{}
1123		}
1124		wd := req.WorktreeDir
1125		if wd == "" {
1126			wd = projectRoot
1127		}
1128	
1129		// Trivial guard — no commits to land.
1130		if req.ResultRev == "" || req.ResultRev == req.BaseRev {
1131			return &LandResult{Status: "no-changes", Reason: "result_rev == base_rev"}, nil
1132		}
1133		syncOperatorAfterLand := sameFilesystemPath(projectRoot, wd)
1134		var operatorDirtyBeforeLand []string
1135		if syncOperatorAfterLand {
1136			operatorDirtyBeforeLand = dirtyWorktreePaths(wd)
1137		}
1138	
1139		// Resolve target branch (default to project HEAD).
1140		targetBranch := req.TargetBranch
1141		if targetBranch == "" {
1142			br, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
1143			if err != nil {
1144				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
1145			}
1146			targetBranch = br
1147		}
1148		req.TargetBranch = targetBranch
1149		if err := ensureLandingWorktreeReady(wd, targetBranch); err != nil {
1150			return nil, err
1151		}
1152		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
1153	
1154		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
1155		if err != nil {
1156			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
1157		}
1158	
1159		contribCount := gitOps.CountCommits(wd, req.BaseRev, req.ResultRev)
1160	
1161		if preserved, err := preserveIfLargeDeletion(wd, req, gitOps, currentTip, contribCount); err != nil {
1162			return nil, err
1163		} else if preserved != nil {
1164			return preserved, nil
1165		}
1166		if preserved, err := preserveIfSyntaxSanityFails(wd, req, gitOps, currentTip, contribCount); err != nil {
1167			return nil, err
1168		} else if preserved != nil {
1169			return preserved, nil
1170		}
1171	
1172		// Fast path: no sibling advanced the target branch → straight ff via
1173		// update-ref. The worker's commit becomes the new tip unchanged; its
1174		// parent is still BaseRev, so replay sees the same inputs the worker
1175		// saw. No merge commit is created.
1176		if currentTip == req.BaseRev {
1177			if err := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, req.ResultRev, currentTip); err != nil {
1178				return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to %s: %w", targetRef, req.ResultRev, err)
1179			}
1180			result := &LandResult{
1181				Status:            "landed",
1182				NewTip:            req.ResultRev,
1183				LandedTip:         req.ResultRev,
1184				TargetBranch:      targetBranch,
1185				Merged:            false,
1186				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1187			}
1188			finalWD, cleanup, err := landingFinalizationWorktree(projectRoot, wd, targetBranch, gitOps)
1189			if err != nil {
1190				return nil, err
1191			}
1192			defer cleanup()
1193			if preserved, err := preserveIfPostLandGateFails(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil); err != nil {
1194				return nil, err
1195			} else if preserved != nil {
1196				return preserved, nil
1197			}
1198			if req.EvidenceDir != "" {
1199				if err := copyEvidenceDirForLanding(projectRoot, finalWD, req.EvidenceDir); err != nil {
1200					return nil, fmt.Errorf("copying evidence into landing worktree: %w", err)
1201				}
1202				if err := rewriteFinalResultArtifactForLand(finalWD, req, result); err != nil {
1203					return nil, fmt.Errorf("rewriting final result artifact: %w", err)
1204				}
1205				if err := landEvidence(finalWD, targetBranch, req, gitOps, result); err != nil {
1206					return preserveAfterEvidenceFailure(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil, err)
1207				}
1208				cleanupProjectEvidenceCopy(projectRoot, req.EvidenceDir)
1209			}
1210			if syncOperatorAfterLand {
1211				syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd, currentTip, operatorDirtyBeforeLand, result)
1212			}
1213			return result, nil
1214		}
1215	
1216		// Merge path: the target has advanced since the worker started. Create
1217		// a temp detached worktree at currentTip and run `git merge --no-ff
1218		// ResultRev` inside it. The result is a merge commit whose parents are
1219		// [currentTip, ResultRev]. Crucially, ResultRev itself is NOT rewritten:
1220		// its parent is still BaseRev, so replay observes the original inputs.
1221		tempWT, tempWtErr := config.MkdirExecutionScratch(projectRoot, "ddx-land-wt-*")
1222		if tempWtErr != nil {
1223			return nil, fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree dir: %w", tempWtErr)
1224		}
1225		// os.MkdirTemp creates the dir, but git worktree add refuses to run if
1226		// the target already exists. Remove it first so git can recreate it.
1227		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
1228		if err := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); err != nil {
1229			return nil, fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, err)
1230		}
1231		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
1232	
1233		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge bead %s attempt %s into %s", req.BeadID, shortAttempt(req.AttemptID), targetBranch)
1234		if err := gitOps.MergeInto(tempWT, req.ResultRev, mergeMsg); err != nil {
1235			// Merge conflict: preserve the original ResultRev and return.
1236			preserveRef := landIterationRef(req.BeadID, req.AttemptID, currentTip)
1237			if upErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, preserveRef, req.ResultRev, ""); upErr != nil {
1238				return nil, fmt.Errorf("preserving %s after merge conflict: %w", preserveRef, upErr)
1239			}
1240			return &LandResult{
1241				Status:            "preserved",
1242				PreserveRef:       preserveRef,
1243				Reason:            "merge conflict",
1244				TargetBranch:      targetBranch,
1245				MergedCommitCount: contribCount,
1246			}, nil

1250		// and fast-forward the target branch to it.

1253			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", err)
1254		}

1256			return nil, fmt.Errorf("fast-forwarding %s to merge commit %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, err)
1257		}
1258		result := &LandResult{

1261			LandedTip:         mergeSHA,
1262			TargetBranch:      targetBranch,
1263			Merged:            true,

1265		}
1266		finalWD, cleanup, err := landingFinalizationWorktree(projectRoot, wd, targetBranch, gitOps)
1267		if err != nil {

1269		}
1270		defer cleanup()
1271		if preserved, err := preserveIfPostLandGateFails(finalWD, req, gitOps, targetRef, currentTip, result.NewTip, contribCount, nil); err != nil {
1272			return nil, err
1273		} else if preserved != nil {
1274			return preserved, nil
1275		}
1276		if req.EvidenceDir != "" {

1409	func largestDeletionFinding(gitOps LandingGitOps, wd, baseRev, resultRev string, threshold int) (largeDeletionFinding, bool, error) {
1410		raw, err := gitOps.DiffNumstat(wd, baseRev, resultRev)
1411		if err != nil {
1412			return largeDeletionFinding{}, false, fmt.Errorf("checking large deletions: %w", err)
1413		}
1414		var largest largeDeletionFinding
1415		for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(raw), "\n") {
1416			if strings.TrimSpace(line) == "" {
1417				continue
1418			}
1419			parts := strings.SplitN(line, "\t", 3)
1420			if len(parts) != 3 || parts[1] == "-" {
1421				continue
1422			}
1423			deleted, parseErr := strconv.Atoi(parts[1])
1424			if parseErr != nil {
1425				continue
1426			}
1427			if deleted > threshold && deleted > largest.Deleted {
1428				largest = largeDeletionFinding{Path: parts[2], Deleted: deleted}
1429			}
1430		}
1431		return largest, largest.Path != "", nil
1432	}

1434	func landHasLargeDeletionAcknowledgement(wd, baseRev, resultRev string) (bool, error) {
1435		out, err := internalgit.Command(context.Background(), wd, "log", "--format=%B", baseRev+".."+resultRev).CombinedOutput()
1436		if err != nil {
1437			return false, fmt.Errorf("checking large-deletion acknowledgement: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)
1438		}
1439		msg := strings.ToLower(string(out))
1440		for _, marker := range []string{
1441			"intentional large deletion",
1442			"intentional file removal",
1443			"intentional file deletion",
1444		} {
1445			if strings.Contains(msg, marker) {
1446				return true, nil
1447			}

1479	func syntaxSanityFindingForResult(gitOps LandingGitOps, wd, baseRev, resultRev string) (syntaxSanityFinding, bool, error) {
1480		paths, err := gitOps.DiffNameOnly(wd, baseRev, resultRev)
1481		if err != nil {
1482			return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, err
1483		}
1484		for _, path := range paths {
1485			result, ok, err := gitFileAt(wd, resultRev, path)
1486			if err != nil {
1487				return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, err
1488			}
1489			if !ok {
1490				continue // deleted files are handled by deletion gates, not syntax checks.
1491			}
1492			base, _, err := gitFileAt(wd, baseRev, path)
1493			if err != nil {
1494				return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, err
1495			}
1496			if reason, bad := syntaxSanityFailure(path, base, result); bad {
1497				return syntaxSanityFinding{Path: path, Reason: reason}, true, nil
1498			}
1499		}
1500		return syntaxSanityFinding{}, false, nil
1501	}

1508			}
1509			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("reading %s at %s: %s: %w", path, rev, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)

1514	func syntaxSanityFailure(path string, base, result []byte) (string, bool) {
1515		switch strings.ToLower(filepath.Ext(path)) {
1516		case ".json":
1517			var v any
1518			if err := json.Unmarshal(result, &v); err != nil {
1519				return "invalid JSON: " + err.Error(), true
1520			}
1521		case ".go":
1522			if _, err := parser.ParseFile(token.NewFileSet(), path, result, parser.AllErrors); err != nil {
1523				return "invalid Go syntax: " + err.Error(), true
1524			}
1525		case ".svelte":
1526			return svelteSanityFailure(base, result)
1527		}
1528		return "", false

1531	func svelteSanityFailure(base, result []byte) (string, bool) {
1532		baseHadScript := bytes.Contains(base, []byte("<script"))
1533		resultHasScript := bytes.Contains(result, []byte("<script"))
1534		resultClosesScript := bytes.Contains(result, []byte("</script>"))
1535		if baseHadScript && (!resultHasScript || !resultClosesScript) {
1536			return "Svelte file lost script structure", true
1537		}
1538		if resultHasScript && !resultClosesScript {
1539			return "Svelte script tag is not closed", true

1544	// LandConflictAutoRecover attempts a 3-way merge of a preserved iteration ref
1545	// onto the current target-branch tip using the ort strategy with -X ours. The
1546	// strategy resolves mechanical conflicts (positional drift from parallel beads)
1547	// by favouring the current tip's version of any conflicting sections while still
1548	// including the preserved iteration's non-conflicting changes in the merge
1549	// commit. If the merge succeeds the local target branch is advanced and the new
1550	// merge commit SHA is returned.
1551	//
1552	// A non-nil error means the ort merge failed (unresolvable content conflict or
1553	// git error) — the caller should escalate to a focused conflict-resolve agent.
1554	// The target branch is never modified on error.
1555	func LandConflictAutoRecover(wd, preserveRef string, gitOps LandingGitOps) (string, error) {
1556		dirtyBefore := dirtyWorktreePaths(wd)
1557		targetBranch, err := gitOps.CurrentBranch(wd)
1558		if err != nil {
1559			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving target branch: %w", err)
1560		}
1561		targetRef := "refs/heads/" + targetBranch
1562	
1563		currentTip, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, targetRef)
1564		if err != nil {
1565			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving target tip %s: %w", targetRef, err)
1566		}
1567	
1568		iterSHA, err := gitOps.ResolveRef(wd, preserveRef)
1569		if err != nil {
1570			return "", fmt.Errorf("resolving preserved ref %s: %w", preserveRef, err)
1571		}
1572	
1573		tempWT, mkErr := config.MkdirExecutionScratch(wd, "ddx-conflict-recover-*")
1574		if mkErr != nil {
1575			return "", fmt.Errorf("creating temp worktree: %w", mkErr)
1576		}
1577		_ = os.RemoveAll(tempWT)
1578		if addErr := gitOps.AddWorktree(wd, tempWT, currentTip); addErr != nil {
1579			return "", fmt.Errorf("adding temp worktree at %s: %w", currentTip, addErr)
1580		}
1581		defer func() { _ = gitOps.RemoveWorktree(wd, tempWT) }()
1582	
1583		mergeMsg := fmt.Sprintf("Merge preserved iteration %s after base drift (ort -X ours)", preserveRef)
1584		out, mergeErr := internalgit.Command(
1585			context.Background(), tempWT,
1586			"-c", "user.name=ddx-land-coordinator",
1587			"-c", "user.email=coordinator@ddx.local",
1588			"merge", "--no-ff", "-s", "ort", "-X", "ours", "-m", mergeMsg, iterSHA,
1589		).CombinedOutput()
1590		if mergeErr != nil {
1591			_ = internalgit.Command(context.Background(), tempWT, "merge", "--abort").Run()
1592			return "", fmt.Errorf("ort merge: %s: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), mergeErr)
1593		}
1594	
1595		mergeSHA, headErr := gitOps.HeadRevAt(tempWT)
1596		if headErr != nil {
1597			return "", fmt.Errorf("reading merge HEAD: %w", headErr)
1598		}
1599		if updErr := gitOps.UpdateRefTo(wd, targetRef, mergeSHA, currentTip); updErr != nil {
1600			return "", fmt.Errorf("advancing %s to %s: %w", targetRef, mergeSHA, updErr)
1601		}
1602		syncWorkTreeToHeadGuarded(gitOps, wd, currentTip, dirtyBefore, nil)
1603		return mergeSHA, nil
1604	}

1606	// shortAttempt returns a 10-char slug derived from attemptID for use in temp
1607	// branch names. When attemptID is empty, it returns the current timestamp.
1608	func shortAttempt(attemptID string) string {
1609		if attemptID != "" {
1610			if len(attemptID) > 16 {
1611				return attemptID[:16]
1612			}
1613			return attemptID

1618	// ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult maps a LandResult onto an
1619	// ExecuteBeadResult so the execute-bead loop sees the correct final status.
1620	// It is the coordinator-pattern replacement for ApplyLandingToResult.
1621	func ApplyLandResultToExecuteBeadResult(res *ExecuteBeadResult, land *LandResult) {
1622		if land == nil || res == nil {
1623			return
1624		}
1625		if land.TargetBranch != "" {
1626			res.TargetBranch = land.TargetBranch
1627		}
1628		switch land.Status {
1629		case "landed":
1630			// Fast-forward or merge commit — either way the target branch now
1631			// contains the worker's result. ResultRev's own parent is still
1632			// BaseRev so replay fidelity is preserved.
1633			reason := ""
1634			res.PreserveRef = ""
1635			if land.Merged {
1636				reason = "merged onto current tip"
1637			}
1638			if land.CheckoutSyncDeferred {
1639				detail := "checkout_sync_deferred"
1640				if len(land.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths) > 0 {
1641					detail += ": " + strings.Join(land.CheckoutSyncDeferredPaths, ", ")
1642				}
1643				if reason == "" {
1644					reason = detail
1645				} else {
1646					reason += "; " + detail
1647				}
1648			}
1649			ApplyLandingToResult(res, &BeadLandingResult{Outcome: "merged", Reason: reason})
1650			landedTip := land.NewTip
1651			if land.LandedTip != "" {
1652				landedTip = land.LandedTip
1653			}
1654			// LandedTip reflects the ref that actually landed the implementation:
1655			// either ResultRev on the ff path or the merge commit SHA on the merge
1656			// path. A trailing final-result/evidence audit commit may advance NewTip
1657			// beyond this landed revision, but callers should keep ResultRev/LandedRev
1658			// pointing at the landed implementation state.
1659			if landedTip != "" {
1660				// Preserve the implementation rev before rewriting the compat alias.
1661				if res.ImplementationRev == "" {
1662					res.ImplementationRev = res.ResultRev
1663				}
1664				res.LandedRev = landedTip
1665				res.ResultRev = landedTip // backwards-compat alias mirrors LandedRev
1666			}
1667		case "preserved":
1668			res.Outcome = "preserved"
1669			res.Reason = land.Reason
1670			res.PreserveRef = land.PreserveRef
1671		case "no-changes":
1672			// Only overwrite when the worker itself did not already report
1673			// a richer no-changes rationale.
1674			if res.Outcome == "" || res.Outcome == ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded {
1675				ApplyLandingToResult(res, &BeadLandingResult{Outcome: "no-changes", Reason: land.Reason})
1676			} else if res.Reason == "" {
1677				res.Reason = land.Reason
1678			}
1679		}
1680		// Re-classify loop-visible status from the landing outcome.
1681		reasonForStatus := res.Reason
1682		if land.Status == "preserved" {
1683			// Route preserve reasons through the land-conflict classifier so the
1684			// loop sees land_conflict (not generic success).
1685			if strings.HasPrefix(res.Reason, PreMergeChecksReason) ||
1686				strings.HasPrefix(res.Reason, "evidence commit failed:") {
1687				reasonForStatus = res.Reason
1688			} else {
1689				reasonForStatus = "merge conflict"
1690			}
1691		}
1692		res.Status = ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(res.Outcome, res.ExitCode, reasonForStatus)
1693		res.OrchestratorStatus = res.Status
1694		res.Detail = ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(res.Status, res.Reason, res.Error)
1695	}
1696	
1697	// BuildLandRequestFromResult constructs a LandRequest for the coordinator from
1698	// an ExecuteBeadResult. The coordinator always passes projectRoot as the
1699	// workdir — the worker's original worktree has already been cleaned up by the
1700	// time Land() runs.
1701	func BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot string, res *ExecuteBeadResult) LandRequest {
1702		// Before the first land, ResultRev points at the candidate that must be
1703		// submitted (either the implementation commit itself or a trailing
1704		// evidence-bundle commit). After a result has already landed once,
1705		// ResultRev is rewritten to the landed branch tip; in that case prefer the
1706		// preserved ImplementationRev so re-submission does not try to land the
1707		// already-landed branch tip.
1708		candidateRev := res.ResultRev
1709		if res.LandedRev != "" && res.ImplementationRev != "" {
1710			candidateRev = res.ImplementationRev
1711		}
1712		if candidateRev == "" {
1713			candidateRev = res.ImplementationRev
1714		}
1715		return LandRequest{
1716			WorktreeDir:  projectRoot,
1717			BaseRev:      res.BaseRev,
1718			ResultRev:    candidateRev,
1719			BeadID:       res.BeadID,
1720			AttemptID:    res.AttemptID,
1721			TargetBranch: "",
1722			EvidenceDir:  res.ExecutionDir,
1723		}
1724	}

47	// findGitRoot walks up from dir to find the directory that contains a .git
48	// entry. Returns dir unchanged if .git cannot be found (graceful fallback).
49	func findGitRoot(dir string) string {
50		current := dir
51		for {
52			if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(current, ".git")); err == nil {
53				return current
54			}
55			parent := filepath.Dir(current)
56			if parent == current {
57				return dir
58			}
59			current = parent
60		}
61	}

63	// IndexLockOwner attempts to identify the pid that currently holds lockPath
64	// open. Uses lsof if available on PATH. Returns (0, nil) if no owner can be
65	// determined. Failure to identify is not itself an error.
66	func IndexLockOwner(lockPath string) (int, error) {
67		lsof, err := exec.LookPath("lsof")
68		if err != nil {
69			return 0, nil
70		}
71		out, err := exec.Command(lsof, "-t", "--", lockPath).Output()
72		if err != nil {
73			// lsof exits non-zero when no process has the file open.
74			return 0, nil
75		}
76		s := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
77		if s == "" {
78			return 0, nil
79		}
80		first := strings.SplitN(s, "\n", 2)[0]
81		pid, perr := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(first))
82		if perr != nil {
83			return 0, nil
84		}
85		return pid, nil
86	}

88	// IndexLockRecoveryResult describes the outcome of one recovery attempt
89	// against .git/index.lock.
90	type IndexLockRecoveryResult struct {
91		// Removed is true if the lock file was deleted.
92		Removed bool
93		// OwnerPID is the pid identified as holding the lock, or 0 if unknown.
94		OwnerPID int
95		// OwnerAlive reports the liveness of OwnerPID at inspection time.
96		// Meaningful only when OwnerPID != 0.
97		OwnerAlive bool
98		// Age is the lock-file age at inspection (zero if lock was missing).
99		Age time.Duration

104	// RecoverGitIndexLock inspects .git/index.lock under projectRoot. If the lock
105	// has no live owner — either lsof identifies a dead pid, or no pid holds it
106	// and the mtime is past StaleAge — the lock is removed. Otherwise the lock is
107	// left in place and the result describes the live owner.
108	//
109	// projectRoot may be a subdirectory of the git repo; RecoverGitIndexLock
110	// walks up to find the actual .git directory so callers that operate on
111	// subdirectories (e.g. .ddx/) still find the correct lock file.
112	func RecoverGitIndexLock(projectRoot string) (IndexLockRecoveryResult, error) {
113		projectRoot = findGitRoot(projectRoot)
114		lockPath := IndexLockPath(projectRoot)
115		info, statErr := os.Lstat(lockPath)
116		if statErr != nil {
117			if os.IsNotExist(statErr) {
118				return IndexLockRecoveryResult{Reason: "lock not present"}, nil
119			}
120			return IndexLockRecoveryResult{}, fmt.Errorf("stat %s: %w", lockPath, statErr)
121		}
122		age := time.Since(info.ModTime())
123		pid, _ := IndexLockOwner(lockPath)
124	
125		if pid > 0 {
126			alive := processAlive(pid)
127			if !alive {
128				if rmErr := os.Remove(lockPath); rmErr != nil && !os.IsNotExist(rmErr) {
129					return IndexLockRecoveryResult{OwnerPID: pid, Age: age},
130						fmt.Errorf("remove lock owned by dead pid %d: %w", pid, rmErr)
131				}
132				return IndexLockRecoveryResult{
133					Removed: true, OwnerPID: pid, OwnerAlive: false, Age: age,
134					Reason: fmt.Sprintf("removed: owner pid %d not alive (lock age %s)", pid, age.Round(time.Second)),
135				}, nil
136			}
137			return IndexLockRecoveryResult{
138				OwnerPID: pid, OwnerAlive: true, Age: age,
139				Reason: fmt.Sprintf("live owner pid %d (lock age %s)", pid, age.Round(time.Second)),
140			}, nil
141		}
142	
143		if age >= StaleAge {
144			if rmErr := os.Remove(lockPath); rmErr != nil && !os.IsNotExist(rmErr) {
145				return IndexLockRecoveryResult{Age: age},
146					fmt.Errorf("remove unowned stale lock (age %s): %w", age, rmErr)
147			}
148			return IndexLockRecoveryResult{
149				Removed: true, Age: age,
150				Reason: fmt.Sprintf("removed: no owner found, age %s >= stale threshold %s",
151					age.Round(time.Second), StaleAge),
152			}, nil
153		}
154	
155		return IndexLockRecoveryResult{
156			Age:    age,
157			Reason: fmt.Sprintf("no owner found but lock is fresh (age %s < %s)", age.Round(time.Second), StaleAge),
158		}, nil
159	}

161	// RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery runs `git args...` in dir and, on
162	// .git/index.lock contention, identifies the owner and either removes a stale
163	// lock or briefly waits for a live owner before retrying.
164	//
165	// Total attempts are bounded by RecoveryAttempts; the final error is decorated
166	// with the most recent recovery diagnostic. The helper deliberately does NOT
167	// loop for tens of seconds — stalling is not acceptable here.
168	func RunGitWithIndexLockRecovery(ctx context.Context, dir string, args ...string) ([]byte, error) {
169		var lastOut []byte
170		var lastErr error
171		var lastDiag string
172		for attempt := 0; attempt < RecoveryAttempts; attempt++ {
173			out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, dir, args...).CombinedOutput()
174			if err == nil {
175				return out, nil
176			}
177			if !IsIndexLockError(string(out)) {
178				return out, err
179			}
180			lastOut = out
181			lastErr = err
182			result, recErr := RecoverGitIndexLock(dir)
183			if recErr != nil {
184				return out, fmt.Errorf("%s; index-lock recovery failed: %w", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), recErr)
185			}
186			lastDiag = result.Reason
187			if !result.Removed {
188				time.Sleep(LiveOwnerWait)
189			}
190		}
191		if lastDiag == "" {
192			return lastOut, lastErr
193		}
194		return lastOut, fmt.Errorf("%s; index-lock owner: %s: %w",
195			strings.TrimSpace(string(lastOut)), lastDiag, lastErr)
196	}

17	// dispatchViaResolvedConfig is the internal SD-024 dispatch seam shared by
18	// execute-bead's worker and the post-merge reviewer. It resolves how the
19	// agent invocation is executed (test-injected runner, pre-built service, or
20	// a fresh service constructed from projectRoot) and routes through
21	// executeOnService so every durable knob lands on the dispatched request
22	// exactly once.
23	//
24	// Resolution order:
25	//  1. runner (test injection seam) — used directly via runner.Run after
26	//     applying any AgentRunRuntime overrides.
27	//  2. svc (pre-built service) — used via executeOnService.
28	//  3. Fallback: construct a fresh service via NewServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)
29	//     and dispatch via executeOnService.
30	//
31	// Override fields on runtime (HarnessOverride, ModelOverride,
32	// PermissionsOverride, SessionLogDirOverride) take precedence over the
33	// matching rcfg accessors when non-empty, so callers can override one knob
34	// for a single invocation without re-resolving the full ResolvedConfig.
35	func dispatchViaResolvedConfig(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, svc agentlib.FizeauService, runner AgentRunner, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
36		if runner != nil {
37			return runner.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
38		}
39		// DDx-local harnesses (virtual, script) are not upstream service harnesses.
40		// Route them through the local Runner so DDX_VIRTUAL_RESPONSES and script
41		// directives work correctly via compare and quorum paths, not just agent run.
42		harness := runtime.HarnessOverride
43		if harness == "" {
44			harness = rcfg.Harness()
45		}
46		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
47			cfg := Config{SessionLogDir: ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, "")}
48			r := NewRunner(cfg)
49			r.WorkDir = projectRoot
50			return r.Run(buildRunArgsFromConfig(ctx, rcfg, runtime))
51		}
52		if svc == nil {
53			factory := serviceRunFactory
54			if factory == nil {
55				factory = NewServiceFromWorkDir
56			}
57			built, err := factory(projectRoot)
58			if err != nil {
59				return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
60			}
61			svc = built
62		}
63		return executeOnService(ctx, svc, projectRoot, rcfg, runtime)
64	}
